---
title: "ACS Variable Availability"
subtitle: "Virginia Counties · 2010–2023"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: dashboard_styles.css
    toc: false
    embed-resources: true
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup

# ── Load packages ─────────────────────────────────────────
# tidyverse: data wrangling (read_csv, mutate, group_by, etc.)
# reactable: interactive HTML tables
# htmltools: build HTML elements like div() in R

library(tidyverse)
library(reactable)
library(htmltools)


# ── Step 1: Load the pre-downloaded ACS data ──────────────
# This CSV was downloaded using tidycensus and contains
# county-level ACS 5-year estimates for Virginia counties
# from 2010 to 2023 for 6 variables.
# Columns: GEOID, NAME, variable, estimate, moe, year

raw <- read_csv("acs_virginia_raw.csv")


# ── Step 2: Define variable metadata ──────────────────────
# ACS variable codes are not human-readable (e.g. B19013_001).
# We create a lookup table that maps each code to:
#   - a plain English label
#   - a thematic category
# This metadata will be joined to the audit results later.

var_metadata <- tibble(
  variable = c("B19013_001", "B17001_002", "B15003_022",
               "B27001_001", "B01003_001", "B28002_004"),
  label    = c("Median Household Income", "Population Below Poverty",
               "Bachelor's Degree", "Health Insurance Universe",
               "Total Population", "Broadband Access"),
  category = c("Socioeconomic", "Socioeconomic", "Socioeconomic",
               "Healthcare Access", "Demographics", "Infrastructure")
)


# ── Step 3: Audit availability from the CSV ───────────────
# Instead of making API calls, we check the CSV directly.
# For each variable, we find which years have data present
# and which years are missing (no rows in the CSV).
#
# distinct() gets us one row per unique variable-year combo
# that actually exists in the data.
# Then we compare against the full 2010-2023 range to find gaps.

years_in_data <- raw |>
  distinct(variable, year)                  # what actually exists

all_combos <- expand_grid(
  variable = unique(raw$variable),          # all 6 variables
  year     = 2010:2023                      # all 14 years
)

audit_results <- all_combos |>
  left_join(years_in_data |> mutate(available = TRUE),
            by = c("variable", "year")) |>
  mutate(available = replace_na(available, FALSE))
# available = TRUE  → data exists for this variable-year
# available = FALSE → data is missing (variable wasn't tracked yet)


# ── Step 4: Summarise into one row per variable ───────────
# We join the human-readable metadata, then collapse all
# 14 year rows per variable into a single summary row showing:
#   n_years       → how many years have data
#   years_present → list of years with data
#   missing_years → list of years without data

audit_final <- audit_results |>
  left_join(var_metadata, by = "variable") |>
  group_by(variable, label, category) |>
  summarise(
    n_years       = sum(available),
    years_present = list(year[available == TRUE]),
    missing_years = list(year[available == FALSE]),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: table

# ── Step 5: Define category colors ────────────────────────
# Each category gets a distinct color used throughout the table.
# These are applied to the Category column text.
# VT maroon and orange for the first two, teal and green for the rest.

cat_colors <- c(
  "Demographics"      = "#861F41",  # VT maroon
  "Socioeconomic"     = "#E5751F",  # VT orange
  "Healthcare Access" = "#2E7D8C",  # teal
  "Infrastructure"    = "#5B7A3A"   # green
)


# ── Step 6: Prepare display-ready table data ──────────────
# audit_final has list columns (years_present, missing_years)
# which don't display well in a table. We convert them to
# human-readable strings here.
#
# coverage     → "7/14" or "14/14"
# missing_text → "—" if none missing, "2010, 2011" if few,
#                "2010–2016" as a range if many
# status       → "Full" or "Partial" for the badge column
#
# n_years is kept hidden — used only for internal logic

table_data <- audit_final |>
  mutate(
    # How many years out of 14 have data
    coverage = paste0(n_years, "/14"),

    # Convert missing_years list to readable string
    missing_text = map_chr(missing_years, function(x) {
      if (length(x) == 0)       "\u2014"                          # — (no missing)
      else if (length(x) <= 3)  paste(x, collapse = ", ")         # e.g. 2010, 2011
      else                      paste0(min(x), "\u2013", max(x))  # e.g. 2010–2016
    }),

    # Simple full/partial flag for the status badge
    status = if_else(n_years == 14, "Full", "Partial")
  ) |>
  select(label, category, coverage, missing_text, status, n_years)


# ── Step 7: Render the interactive reactable table ────────
# reactable() creates an interactive HTML table with:
#   searchable  → search box above the table
#   filterable  → dropdown filters on each column
#   highlight   → row highlights on hover
#
# reactableTheme() controls the overall table appearance:
#   headerStyle → column header background and text
#   borderColor → line between rows
#   cellPadding → spacing inside each cell
#
# Each colDef() controls one column:
#   name  → display name shown in header
#   width → column width in pixels
#   style → fixed or data-driven cell styling
#   cell  → custom HTML rendering (used for status badge)
#   show  → FALSE hides the column from display

reactable(
  table_data,
  searchable      = TRUE,
  filterable      = TRUE,
  highlight       = TRUE,
  defaultPageSize = 10,

  # Overall table font
  style = list(fontFamily = "Calibri, Arial, sans-serif",
               fontSize   = "15px"),

  # Table-level theme — colors, borders, padding
  theme = reactableTheme(
    headerStyle    = list(
      background = "#861F41",   # VT maroon header
      color      = "white",
      fontWeight = "bold",
      fontSize   = "13px"
    ),
    borderColor    = "#e0d6cf", # soft border between rows
    stripedColor   = "#faf5f6", # alternating row shading
    highlightColor = "#f3eeea", # hover row color
    cellPadding    = "10px 16px"
  ),

  columns = list(

    # Variable name — plain dark text, slightly bold
    label = colDef(
      name  = "Variable",
      width = 240,
      style = list(fontWeight = "500", color = "#1a0a0f")
    ),

    # Category — colored by category using cat_colors lookup
    category = colDef(
      name  = "Category",
      width = 180,
      style = function(value) {
        list(color = cat_colors[value], fontWeight = "bold")
      }
    ),

    # Coverage — green if full (14/14), orange if partial
    coverage = colDef(
      name  = "Coverage",
      width = 100,
      align = "center",
      style = function(value) {
        if (value == "14/14") list(color = "#2E7D8C", fontWeight = "bold")
        else                  list(color = "#E5751F", fontWeight = "bold")
      }
    ),

    # Missing years — grey dash if none, maroon text if missing
    missing_text = colDef(
      name  = "Missing Years",
      width = 160,
      align = "center",
      style = function(value) {
        if (value == "\u2014") list(color = "#8a7070")  # grey dash
        else                   list(color = "#861F41", fontWeight = "500")
      }
    ),

    # Status badge — pill-shaped colored label
    # Full = teal badge, Partial = orange badge
    status = colDef(
      name  = "Status",
      width = 110,
      align = "center",
      cell  = function(value) {
        color <- if (value == "Full") "#2E7D8C" else "#E5751F"
        bg    <- if (value == "Full") "#e8f4f6" else "#fdf0e8"
        div(style = paste0(
          "display:inline-block; padding:3px 10px; border-radius:100px;",
          "font-size:11px; font-weight:600;",
          "color:", color, "; background:", bg, ";"
        ), value)
      }
    ),

    # n_years — hidden, used only for coverage/status logic above
    n_years = colDef(show = FALSE)
  )
)
a```
